zh.lifeSummary: 當Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg於大約1300年出生在丹麥利爾斯廷格列福時，他的父親Peder Jernskjaeg32歲，母親Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg32歲。他於大約1329年與Karen Andersdatter結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1366年之後在丹麥索洛林斯泰德瓦爾奧利勒，埋葬在挪威特洛姆斯吉可加奧。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg于大约1300年出生在丹麦利爾斯廷格列福时，他的父亲Peder Jernskjaeg32岁，母亲Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg32岁。他于大约1329年与Karen Andersdatter结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1366年之后在丹麦索洛林斯泰德瓦爾奧利勒，埋葬在挪威特洛姆斯吉可加奧。

nameMeanings.name: Niels
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

nameMeanings.name: Pedersen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Niels, Bjorn, Knud, Nils, Anders, Jorgen, Egil, Einer, Knut, Peer.
Norwegian and Danish: patronymic from the personal name Peder (see Peter ). This surname is also found in northern Germany. Compare Petersen 2 and Peterson 1.

variants.name: Pederson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Hjalmer, Obert, Ove, Hilmer, Iver, Knud, Lars, Selmer, Alf, Anders.
Americanized form of Norwegian and Danish Pedersen or of the Swedish cognate Pedersson.

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg was born about 1300, in Tinglev, Riberhus, Denmark, his father, Peder Jernskjaeg, was 32 and his mother, Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg, was 32. He married Karen Andersdatter about 1329. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died after 1366, in Valsølille, Ringsted, Sorø, Denmark, and was buried in Kirkegardøy, Troms, Norway.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg est né environ 1300, à Tinglev, Riberhus, Danemark, son père, Peder Jernskjaeg, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Karen Andersdatter environ 1329. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé après 1366, à Valsølille, Ringsted, Sorø, Danemark, et a été enterré à Kirkegardøy, Troms, Norvège.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg um 1300 in Tinglev, Riberhus, Dänemark geboren wurde, war sein Vater Peder Jernskjaeg 32 und seine Mutter Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg 32. Er hat um 1329 Karen Andersdatter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb nach 1366 in Valsølille, Ringsted, Sorø, Dänemark und wurde in Kirkegardøy, Troms, Norwegen begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg è nato il circa 1300 nel Tinglev, Riberhus, Danimarca, il suo padre, Peder Jernskjaeg, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Karen Andersdatter circa 1329. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì dopo il 1366, nel Valsølille, Ringsted, Sorø, Danimarca, e fu sepolto a Kirkegardøy, Troms, Norvegia.

ja.lifeSummary: Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjægが約1300年にデンマークリバーハスティングレフで生まれた時，父Peder Jernskjaegは32歳，母Mrs. Peder Jernskjaegは32歳であった。約1329年にKaren Andersdatterと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1366年以後にデンマークソルoリングステドヴァルスoリルで死去し，ノルウェートロムズキルケガードoイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg(이)가 대략 1300년 덴마크 리버후스 틴글레프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Peder Jernskjaeg(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1329년 Karen Andersdatter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1366년 이후 덴마크 소o 링스테드 발스o리에서 사망했으며 노르웨이 트롬스 커케가드o이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg nasceu aproximadamente 1300, em Tinglev, Riberhus, Dinamarca, seu pai, Peder Jernskjaeg, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Karen Andersdatter aproximadamente 1329. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu após 1366, em Valsølille, Ringsted, Sorø, Dinamarca, e foi sepultado em Kirkegardøy, Troms, Noruega.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg родился около 1300 г. в местности тинглев, рибирус, Дания, его отцу по имени Peder Jernskjaeg было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Karen Andersdatter около 1329 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер после 1366 г. в местности валсoлилл, рингстед, сорo, Дания и был похоронен в местности киркегардoи, тромс, Норвегия.

sm.ABT 1300–after 1366: e tusa o le 1300–ina ua ma'ea le 1366

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adelsmand Niels Pedersen Jernskjæg nació aproximadamente 1300, en Tinglev, Riberhus, Dinamarca, su papá, Peder Jernskjaeg, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Mrs. Peder Jernskjaeg, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Karen Andersdatter aproximadamente 1329. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió después de 1366, en Valsølille, Ringsted, Sorø, Dinamarca, y fue sepultado en Kirkegardøy, Troms, Noruega.
